test_cases.test_case: Successful sign in with valid credentials
preconditions: Registered user exists with <valid email address or username> and <valid password> that meets password complexity requirements
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Enter <valid email address or username> in the Email/Username field
3. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
4. Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and redirects to Accounts Overview page

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Forgot Password page via Forgot Password? link
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Click the Forgot Password? link
test_cases.expected_result: Navigates to Forgot Password page

test_cases.test_case: Successful registration with all required fields completed
steps: 1. Enter <first name> in the First Name field
2. Enter <last name> in the Last Name field
3. Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
4. Enter <city> in the City field
5. Select <state> from the State dropdown
6. Enter <ZIP code in 5-digit format> in the ZIP Code field
7. Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
8. Enter <SSN> in the SSN field
9. Enter <valid email address> in the Username field
10. Enter <password> in the Password field
11. Enter <password> in the Confirm Password field
12. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Account created successfully — please sign in; redirects to login page

test_cases.test_case: Phone number input is automatically formatted to (123) 456-7890
steps: 1. Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
2. Move focus away from the Phone Number field
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number field displays the input formatted as (123) 456-7890 (automatic formatting applied)

test_cases.test_case: SSN input is automatically formatted to 123-45-6789
steps: 1. Enter <SSN> in the SSN field
2. Move focus away from the SSN field
test_cases.expected_result: SSN field displays the input formatted as 123-45-6789 (automatic formatting applied)

test_cases.test_case: ZIP Code field accepts 5+4 format (12345-6789)
steps: 1. Enter <ZIP code in 5+4 format> in the ZIP Code field
2. Move focus away from the ZIP Code field
test_cases.expected_result: ZIP Code field accepts and displays the entered 5+4 format (12345-6789)

test_cases.test_case: State dropdown allows selecting a US state
steps: 1. Click the State dropdown
2. Select <state> from the State dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: State dropdown shows <state> as selected

test_cases.test_case: Accounts Overview displays welcome message, masked account numbers, status badges, ordered rows and total balance
preconditions: At least two customer accounts exist with differing <open date> and <current balance> values
steps: 1. Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: The page displays a welcome message that includes <user name>. The accounts table is visible with columns: Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status, and Open Date. Account Number values are masked and show only the last 4 digits (format: ****5001). Account Status shows an 'Active' badge for active accounts. Rows are ordered by Open Date ascending (earliest first). The table footer shows the label "Total Balance" and the displayed total equals the sum of the Current Balance values shown in the table.

test_cases.test_case: Account Number rendered as clickable element but click action not implemented
preconditions: At least one customer account exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
2. Click the Account Number in the first row of the accounts table
test_cases.expected_result: The Account Number in the row is rendered as a clickable element (for example, link or button). Clicking the Account Number does not navigate away from the Accounts Overview page or open account details because the click action is not implemented; the Accounts Overview table remains visible.

test_cases.test_case: Open Checking account with valid initial deposit and sufficient funding
preconditions: Funding account <funding account with sufficient balance> exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Open New Account page
2. Select the Checking account type card
3. Enter <initial deposit amount meeting Checking minimum> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select <funding account with sufficient balance> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
5. Click the Open Account button
test_cases.expected_result: "Account opened successfully!" is displayed and the user is redirected to Accounts Overview

test_cases.test_case: Open Savings account with valid initial deposit and sufficient funding
preconditions: Funding account <funding account with sufficient balance> exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Open New Account page
2. Select the Savings account type card
3. Enter <initial deposit amount meeting Savings minimum> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select <funding account with sufficient balance> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
5. Click the Open Account button
test_cases.expected_result: "Account opened successfully!" is displayed and the user is redirected to Accounts Overview

test_cases.test_case: Account type selection updates displayed minimum deposit requirement
steps: 1. Navigate to the Open New Account page
2. Select the Checking account type card
3. Select the Savings account type card
test_cases.expected_result: The account type card display updates to show the minimum deposit requirement for the selected type (shows $25 for Checking and $100 for Savings)

test_cases.test_case: Open Account button becomes enabled when all required fields have valid values
preconditions: Funding account <funding account with sufficient balance> exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Open New Account page
2. Select an account type
3. Enter <initial deposit amount meeting selected account type minimum> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select <funding account with sufficient balance> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The Open Account button is enabled when Account Type is selected, Initial Deposit Amount is numeric and meets the selected type's minimum, and a funding account with sufficient balance is selected

test_cases.test_case: Internal transfer (My ParaBank Account) happy path
preconditions: <source account> has sufficient funds for <transfer amount>
User has <internal destination account> in their accounts
steps: 1. Navigate to the Transfer page
2. Select "My ParaBank Account" in the Transfer Type radio buttons
3. Click the Source Account dropdown
4. Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
5. Click the Internal Destination Account dropdown
6. Select <internal destination account> from the Internal Destination Account dropdown
7. Enter <transfer amount> in the Transfer Amount field
8. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer completed successfully. Returns transaction ID. A transaction ID is displayed on screen.

test_cases.test_case: External transfer (External Account) happy path with matching account numbers
preconditions: <source account> has sufficient funds for <transfer amount>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Transfer page
2. Select "External Account" in the Transfer Type radio buttons
3. Click the Source Account dropdown
4. Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
5. Enter <external account number> in the External Account Number field
6. Enter <external account number> in the External Account Number Confirmation field
7. Enter <transfer amount> in the Transfer Amount field
8. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer completed successfully. Returns transaction ID. A transaction ID is displayed on screen.

test_cases.test_case: Source Account dropdown is filtered to Checking and Savings accounts
preconditions: User has multiple accounts including accounts of type Checking and Savings
steps: 1. Navigate to the Transfer page
2. Click the Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Source Account dropdown lists only accounts of type Checking or Savings

test_cases.test_case: Submit payment with matching account numbers and sufficient funds
preconditions: Source account with available funds >= <payment amount>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Bill Payment page
2. Enter <payee name> in the Payee Name field
3. Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
4. Enter <city> in the City field
5. Enter <state> in the State field
6. Enter <zip code> in the ZIP Code field
7. Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
8. Enter <payee account number> in the Payee Account Number field
9. Enter <payee account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
10. Enter <payment amount> in the Payment Amount field
11. Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
12. Click the Pay button
test_cases.expected_result: Payment submitted successfully. Returns reference code and updates balances. A payment reference code is displayed to the user. Source account and payee balances are updated to reflect the deduction and credit of <payment amount> respectively.

test_cases.test_case: Submit payment where source account funds equal the payment amount (boundary condition)
preconditions: Source account with available funds equal to <payment amount>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Bill Payment page
2. Enter <payee name> in the Payee Name field
3. Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
4. Enter <city> in the City field
5. Enter <state> in the State field
6. Enter <zip code> in the ZIP Code field
7. Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
8. Enter <payee account number> in the Payee Account Number field
9. Enter <payee account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
10. Enter <payment amount> in the Payment Amount field
11. Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
12. Click the Pay button
test_cases.expected_result: Payment submitted successfully. Returns reference code and updates balances. A payment reference code is displayed to the user. Source account balance is reduced by <payment amount> (resulting balance may be zero) and the payee's balance is increased by <payment amount>.

test_cases.test_case: Request Personal Loan without Collateral (happy path)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Request Loan page
2. Select Personal in the Loan Type radio group
3. Enter <valid personal loan amount within Personal range> in the Loan Amount field
4. Enter <valid down payment amount meeting minimum 10% requirement and less than Loan Amount> in the Down Payment Amount field
5. Ensure the Collateral Account dropdown is left unselected
6. Click the Request Loan button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! (no actual balance debits occur)  is displayed and the newly created loan is visible with <loan details> including Loan Type set to Personal and the entered Loan Amount and Down Payment

test_cases.test_case: Request Auto Loan with Collateral meeting 20% requirement (happy path)
preconditions: <Collateral account with sufficient available funds and collateral value >= 20% of Loan Amount> exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Request Loan page
2. Select Auto in the Loan Type radio group
3. Enter <valid auto loan amount within Auto range> in the Loan Amount field
4. Enter <valid down payment amount meeting minimum 10% requirement and less than Loan Amount> in the Down Payment Amount field
5. Select <collateral account with sufficient available funds and collateral value >= 20% of Loan Amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
6. Click the Request Loan button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! (no actual balance debits occur)  is displayed and the newly created loan is visible with <loan details> including Loan Type set to Auto and the selected Collateral Account shown

test_cases.test_case: Request Home Loan without Collateral (happy path)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Request Loan page
2. Select Home in the Loan Type radio group
3. Enter <valid home loan amount within Home range> in the Loan Amount field
4. Enter <valid down payment amount meeting minimum 10% requirement and less than Loan Amount> in the Down Payment Amount field
5. Ensure the Collateral Account dropdown is left unselected
6. Click the Request Loan button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! (no actual balance debits occur)  is displayed and the newly created loan is visible with <loan details> including Loan Type set to Home and the entered Loan Amount and Down Payment

test_cases.test_case: Request Home Loan with Collateral meeting 20% requirement (happy path)
preconditions: <Collateral account with sufficient available funds and collateral value >= 20% of Loan Amount> exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Request Loan page
2. Select Home in the Loan Type radio group
3. Enter <valid home loan amount within Home range> in the Loan Amount field
4. Enter <valid down payment amount meeting minimum 10% requirement and less than Loan Amount> in the Down Payment Amount field
5. Select <collateral account with sufficient available funds and collateral value >= 20% of Loan Amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
6. Click the Request Loan button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! (no actual balance debits occur)  is displayed and the newly created loan is visible with <loan details> including Loan Type set to Home and the selected Collateral Account shown

test_cases.test_case: Update all profile fields with valid data and submit
preconditions: Customer profile page open with pre-filled data
steps: 1. Navigate to the Customer Profile page
2. Enter <first name> in the First Name field
3. Enter <last name> in the Last Name field
4. Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
5. Enter <city> in the City field
6. Select <state> in the State field
7. Enter <zip code> in the ZIP Code field
8. Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
9. Click the Update Profile button
test_cases.expected_result: Profile updated successfully. Refreshes the data. The form displays the updated values for First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, and Phone Number and no inline error banner is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Verify profile form is pre-filled with existing customer data
steps: 1. Navigate to the Customer Profile page
test_cases.expected_result: All fields (First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Phone Number) are pre-filled with the customer's existing values.

test_cases.test_case: Submit profile without making changes (pre-filled values) and confirm success
preconditions: Customer profile page open with pre-filled data
steps: 1. Navigate to the Customer Profile page
2. Click the Update Profile button
test_cases.expected_result: Profile updated successfully. Refreshes the data. The form remains populated with the existing customer values and no inline error banner is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Update address fields only and submit successfully
preconditions: Customer profile page open with pre-filled data
steps: 1. Navigate to the Customer Profile page
2. Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
3. Enter <city> in the City field
4. Select <state> in the State field
5. Enter <zip code> in the ZIP Code field
6. Click the Update Profile button
test_cases.expected_result: Profile updated successfully. Refreshes the data. The form displays the updated Street Address, City, State, and ZIP Code values and no inline error banner is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Update phone number only and submit successfully
preconditions: Customer profile page open with pre-filled data
steps: 1. Navigate to the Customer Profile page
2. Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
3. Click the Update Profile button
test_cases.expected_result: Profile updated successfully. Refreshes the data. The form displays the updated Phone Number and no inline error banner is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Submit Card Request with complete address and eligible account
preconditions: An account in good standing exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Manage Cards page
2. Select <card type> from the Card Type dropdown
3. Select <account to link> from the Account to Link field
4. Enter <complete shipping address> in the Shipping Address field
5. Click the Request Card button
test_cases.expected_result: opens card-request ticket and shows 'Card request submitted successfully.' with tracking ID

test_cases.test_case: Update spending limit within policy for an existing card
steps: 1. Navigate to the Manage Cards page
2. Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
3. Enter <new spending limit within policy> in the New Spending Limit field
4. Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: Card controls updated successfully.

test_cases.test_case: Add a valid travel notice with start/end dates and destinations
steps: 1. Navigate to the Manage Cards page
2. Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
3. Enter <start date> in the Travel Notice Start Date field
4. Enter <end date> in the Travel Notice End Date field
5. Enter <destinations> in the Travel Notice Destinations field
6. Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: Card controls updated successfully. No inline validation errors for Travel Notice; end date is on or after start date.

test_cases.test_case: Change card status and verify resulting status is applied
preconditions: A card in <current status> status exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Manage Cards page
2. Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
3. Select <new status> from the Card Status dropdown
4. Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: Card controls updated successfully. Card Status displays <new status>.

test_cases.test_case: Execute Buy trade successfully
steps: 1. Navigate to the Investments page
2. In the Trade Funds form, select Buy from the Action dropdown
3. In the Trade Funds form, enter <Fund Symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
4. In the Trade Funds form, enter <Quantity> (greater than 0) in the Quantity field
5. In the Trade Funds form, select <Funding Account> from the Funding or Destination Account dropdown
6. Click the Execute Trade button
test_cases.expected_result: executes same-day trade; updates holdings; displays "Trade executed successfully." with order ID

test_cases.test_case: Execute Sell trade successfully
preconditions: <account with sufficient share balance of the specified Fund_Symbol>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Investments page
2. In the Trade Funds form, select Sell from the Action dropdown
3. In the Trade Funds form, enter <Fund Symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
4. In the Trade Funds form, enter <Quantity> (greater than 0 and less than or equal to share balance) in the Quantity field
5. In the Trade Funds form, select <Destination Account> from the Funding or Destination Account dropdown
6. Click the Execute Trade button
test_cases.expected_result: executes same-day trade; updates holdings; displays "Trade executed successfully." with order ID

test_cases.test_case: Create recurring investment plan successfully
preconditions: <funding account with adequate balance for the first contribution>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Investments page
2. In the Recurring Investment Plan form, enter <Fund Symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
3. In the Recurring Investment Plan form, enter <Contribution Amount> (meets minimum contribution) in the Contribution Amount field
4. In the Recurring Investment Plan form, select Monthly from the Frequency dropdown
5. In the Recurring Investment Plan form, enter <Start Date in the future> in the Start Date field
6. In the Recurring Investment Plan form, select <Funding Account> from the Funding Account dropdown
7. Click the Create Plan button
test_cases.expected_result: stores schedule; displays "Plan created successfully."

test_cases.test_case: Investments workspace shows portfolio snapshot and both forms
steps: 1. Navigate to the Investments page
2. Verify the portfolio snapshot panel is visible
3. Verify the portfolio snapshot displays current fund holdings
4. Verify the portfolio snapshot displays market value
5. Verify the portfolio snapshot displays unrealised gain or loss
6. Verify the Trade Funds form is visible
7. Verify the Trade Funds form contains Action, Fund Symbol, Quantity, Funding or Destination Account fields and an Execute Trade button
8. Verify the Recurring Investment Plan form is visible
9. Verify the Recurring Investment Plan form contains Fund Symbol, Contribution Amount, Frequency, Start Date, Funding Account fields and a Create Plan button
test_cases.expected_result: Portfolio snapshot is visible and shows current fund holdings, market value, and unrealised gain or loss; Trade Funds form and Recurring Investment Plan form are visible with their respective fields and buttons

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement using Custom Date Range
steps: 1. Select "Custom Date Range" for the Statement Period
2. Enter <start date> in the Start Date field
3. Enter <end date> in the End Date field
4. Select <account> from the Account dropdown
5. Click the Generate Statement button
test_cases.expected_result: retrieves relevant transactions and displays "Statement generated successfully."

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement using Month-and-Year selection
steps: 1. Select "Month-and-Year" for the Statement Period
2. Select <account> from the Account dropdown
3. Click the Generate Statement button
test_cases.expected_result: retrieves relevant transactions and displays "Statement generated successfully."

test_cases.test_case: Save e-Statement preference with paperless opt-in
steps: 1. Click the Paperless_Opt_In checkbox to opt in to paperless statements
2. Enter <email address> in the Email Address field
3. Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: displays "e-Statement preference updated."

test_cases.test_case: Verify both forms are visible side by side on the Statements page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Statements page
2. Observe that the Statement Period radio and the Generate Statement button are visible
3. Observe that the Account dropdown is visible
4. Observe that the Paperless_Opt_In checkbox, Email Address field, and Save Preference button are visible
test_cases.expected_result: Both forms are visible side by side: Statement Period, Account, Generate Statement; Paperless_Opt_In, Email Address, Save Preference are present.

test_cases.test_case: End-to-end: generate statement then update e-Statement preference
steps: 1. Select "Custom Date Range" for the Statement Period
2. Enter <start date> in the Start Date field
3. Enter <end date> in the End Date field
4. Select <account> from the Account dropdown
5. Click the Generate Statement button
6. Click the Paperless_Opt_In checkbox to opt in to paperless statements
7. Enter <email address> in the Email Address field
8. Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: retrieves relevant transactions and displays "Statement generated successfully." Then displays "e-Statement preference updated."

test_cases.test_case: Change password happy path
steps: 1. Navigate to the Security Settings page
2. Click the Toggle Panel control on the Change Password panel to expand it
3. Enter <current password> in the Current Password field
4. Enter <strong password> in the New Password field
5. Enter <strong password> in the Confirm New Password field
6. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Credentials are updated and the success message "Password changed successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Change Password panel expand and collapse
steps: 1. Navigate to the Security Settings page
2. Click the Toggle Panel control on the Change Password panel to expand it
3. Verify the Change Password form is visible and shows fields Current Password, New Password, Confirm New Password
4. Click the Toggle Panel control on the Change Password panel to collapse it
5. Verify the Change Password form is hidden
test_cases.expected_result: The Change Password panel expands to reveal the form with fields Current Password, New Password, Confirm New Password, and collapses to hide the form when toggled.

test_cases.test_case: Send secure message with subject, category, rich-text body and valid attachment
steps: 1. Navigate to the Support Center page
2. Enter <subject> in the Subject field
3. Select <Category> from the Category dropdown
4. Enter <message body> in the Message Body field
5. Upload <valid attachment file> to the Attachment field
6. Click the Send Message button
test_cases.expected_result: Message sent successfully. Returns ticket ID.

test_cases.test_case: Request callback with preferred date at least next business day
steps: 1. Navigate to the Support Center page
2. Enter <preferred date at least next business day> in the Preferred Date field
3. Enter <preferred time window> in the Preferred Time Window field
4. Click the Request Callback button
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted. Sends email confirmation.

test_cases.test_case: Phone Number field is pre-filled from user profile in Schedule Callback form
steps: 1. Navigate to the Support Center page
2. Observe the Phone Number field in the Schedule Callback form
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number field is pre-filled with <phone number from user profile>.

test_cases.test_case: Category dropdown displays all expected options
steps: 1. Navigate to the Support Center page
2. Open the Category dropdown in the Secure Message form
test_cases.expected_result: Category dropdown displays the options Account, Technical, Security, and Other.
